description:
Two sets of inconsistent duplications:

1. Frontend keyword detection:
- OrchestratorTask::is_frontend_related in types.rs:1064-1124
- requirement_is_frontend_related in planning_shared.rs:240-294
Both scan tags for ["frontend", "ui", "ux", "design", "react", ...] and haystack for ["user interface", "user experience", ...] but with different keyword sets (task adds "tailwind", "storybook"; requirement adds "wireframe", "mockup").

2. ComplexityTier value maps:
- planning_shared.rs:419-421 — Simple=>(1,2), Medium=>(2,4), Complex=>(3,6)
- planning_utils/complexity.rs:106-108 — Simple=>(4-8, Low), Medium=>(8-14, Medium), Complex=>(14-30, High)
- planning_utils/requirements.rs:246-248 — Simple=>2, Medium=>3, Complex=>usize::MAX
- planning_utils/mod.rs:82-84 — Simple=>"simple", Medium=>"medium", Complex=>"complex" (no Display impl)

Four different tier-to-values maps for the same enum. Should be methods on ComplexityTier.

Also: next_task_id / next_requirement_id are structurally identical (task_shared.rs:4 vs planning_utils/mod.rs:11). A generic next_sequential_id(keys, prefix) would unify them.
title: Deduplicate frontend-related keyword sets and ComplexityTier value maps
